item.title: Re: Based off SCP CB?
item.content:encoded: That was just about what I was going to ask. It doesn't appear to be, because i feel if it was to be related to the video then it would either allude to D-9341's ability to "respawn" after dying or something else in batesee's video. My memory is still a bit rusty, and I haven't watched it in a while. It's still a theory, and batesee's theory is also a huge leap from what the devs might consider canon

item.title: Based off SCP CB?
item.content:encoded: Is this based off the whole spiral gestalt thing described in TheBatesee's video? and also from the game?

item.title: Re: Author Post - The Spiral Gestalt
item.content:encoded:
I've decided to change the doctor in the skip from my author avatar to a character made just for the purpose of this skip because of a series that I plan on making with this skip; since my author avatar is already in another WIP series, I removed it from here to avoid confusion in the future.

Hope I didn't cause any confusion in the process!

item.title: Re: Author Post - The Spiral Gestalt
item.content:encoded:
The Gestalt as a whole is just one of the two things that inspired me to make this article.

e: I was rereading the article and saw where your confusion came in, so I went ahead and edited a few things for clarification.

item.title: Re: Author Post - The Spiral Gestalt
item.content:encoded:
So if Caroll never thinks it have anything to do with the Gestalt1 why did you call it that?

Footnotes
1. That may not be theorized yet.

item.title: Re: Author Post - The Spiral Gestalt
item.content:encoded:
I think you might be confused.

Spoilers for those who haven't read
– hide block

The entire article until you get to the "[ACCESS SCP-4598?]" part, including the containment procedures you're talking about, was written by Caroll Hammond alone when he himself found out about the anomaly by being the first (and only) person affected by it, being transported to this new location. The new document found at the very bottom after you access the file is written by the Foundation. Also, there's nowhere in the article itself that calls it the Spiral Gestalt, that's just my title for the skip out-of-universe.

item.title: Re: Author Post - The Spiral Gestalt
item.content:encoded: I get it was Caroll's and not the Foundation as a whole's but how why did he start out calling it SCP-4598 and then ~6 years later the Foundation as a whole called it the "The Spiral Gestalt" and made it a highly restricted field of research?

item.title: Re: Author Post - The Spiral Gestalt
item.content:encoded:
I moreso used the phrase "the Spiral Gestalt" as diving headfirst into madness ultimately leading you to perceive (and straight up experience) reality differently which is exactly what Caroll Hammond experienced in the article, all because of some song lyrics he just thought too hard about.

e: Also, the first description and object class and stuff wasn't written nor approved by the Foundation. It was written by Dr. Caroll Hammond himself as he started to experience these things, as shown at the very ending by it being in Caroll's Hammond's terminal.

item.title: Re: Author Post - The Spiral Gestalt
item.content:encoded: Am I missing something or does none of this other than the note relate to The Spiral Gestalt? Roughly six years later it's still "hypothetical anomalous phenomenon" and not an SCP item. Did they stop calling it "SCP-4598" and go back to a "hypothetical anomalous phenomenon" at some point?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Interesting. You somehow managed to "It was all a dream" twice in the same medium-length article. +1

item.title: Author Post - The Spiral Gestalt
item.content:encoded:
This is my entry for the 144-Hour Jam Contest, Theme Three.

Image is CC-compliant.

You can enjoy more of my articles here.

Special thanks to Ihp for their critique, and thank you for reading!

And thanks to pieiswrong for authorization of usage of part of their JamCon entry, which is the entry that straight up inspired me to right this one. No, we did not collaborate in each other's entries at all; I just read his and it, alongside the Spiral Gestalt, inspired me to create this.
